Neither owns cultural-object material condition, treatment compatibility, reversibility, intervention history, stabilization, and display or storage readiness; live inventory found no conservation owner.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until multi-material compatibility, reversible interventions, snapshot migration, deterministic replay, and two artifact classes pass.

acceptedKits.name: circadian-entrainment-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Advance authored biological clock profiles through internal phase, explicit zeitgeber observations, entrainment, phase shifts, desynchronization, adaptation, and activity-readiness windows without owning fatigue or schedules.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns timers, agents, deterministic state, and observations; this domain owns biological phase, zeitgeber response, entrainment strength, desynchronization, phase adaptation, and circadian readiness meaning.

ownership: subject internal phase and cycle length
dependencies: stable profile, subject, zeitgeber, and phase-shift IDs
authored biological cycle and response profiles
explicit light, temperature, meal, or social timing observations
plausibleUses: rotate a habitat crew through a long artificial day without losing synchronized activity windows
guide nocturnal pollinators through a moonless seasonal shift
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Longnight Habitat route must replay zeitgebers, phase shifts, fixed ticks, readiness checks, and adaptation commands into identical phase, offset, entrainment, desynchronization, readiness, and rejection state across human-shift and nocturnal-species profiles.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Zeitgeber, phase-shift, readiness, and adaptation IDs commit once, phase advances only from explicit ticks in stable subject order, and readiness events emit only on window transitions.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Crew fatigue and actor-fatigue candidates own exertion and recovery, NPC schedules own planned activities, and phenology owns seasonal biological stages. None owns internal daily phase, zeitgeber entrainment, desynchronization, adaptation, and circadian readiness; live inventory found no circadian owner.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Hold until different cycle lengths, competing zeitgebers, snapshot migration, long replay, and two biological subject classes pass.

acceptedKits.name: environmental-trace-persistence-domain-kit
acceptedKits.purpose: Track authored physical traces left in the world through emission, spatial anchoring, aging, intensity decay, disturbance, masking, contamination, transfer, and terminal disappearance without owning perception or investigation conclusions.

coreMapping.nonDuplicatedMeaning: Core owns scene identities, spatial facts, agents, timers, and ledgers; this domain owns environmental trace emission lineage, persistence, age, intensity, disturbance, masking, contamination, transfer, and disappearance meaning.

ownership: portable spatial anchors and affected media
trace age, intensity, and decay state
disturbance, masking, contamination, and transfer history
active, ambiguous, and disappeared trace classification
snapshotsAndDescriptors: trace source, medium, and anchor descriptors
age, intensity, decay, and transfer descriptors
disturbance, masking, contamination, ambiguity, and disappearance summaries
dependencies: stable source, trace, anchor, disturbance, and transfer IDs
authored trace-medium response and decay profiles
explicit contact, environment, and disturbance observations
plausibleUses: track missing wildlife across rain, mud, and crowded crossings before signs disappear
follow a masked machine trail through a damaged station while contamination creates false leads
acceptedKits.experimentProof: A seeded Whitewood Pursuit route must replay emissions, weather-like disturbance facts, masking, contamination, transfers, and fixed decay ticks into identical lineage, intensity, ambiguity, disappearance, and rejection state across wildlife and machine traces.
acceptedKits.idempotencyRule: Emission, disturbance, mask, contamination, transfer, and removal IDs commit once, decay advances only from explicit ticks in stable trace order, and disappeared traces cannot transfer or decay again.
acceptedKits.duplicateAndMergeRisk: Sensory-agent and perception kits own observations and detection, investigation owns evidence and hypotheses, and vegetation footprints or decals are presentation facts. None owns persistent trace lineage, medium-dependent decay, disturbance, masking, contamination, transfer, and disappearance; live inventory found no environmental-trace owner.
acceptedKits.promotionPotential: Experimental until dense-trace scale, mixed-media transfer, snapshot migration, long replay, and wildlife plus machine trace profiles pass.

rejectedCandidates.name: signal-triangulation-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The existing universal game-domain inventory already declares triangulation-kit inside survey/cartography map coverage, and acoustic-signal plus scan/survey families already own adjacent signal observations.

rejectedCandidates.name: investigation-evidence-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: semantic-bounded-domain-kits already installs engine.n.investigation and owns cases, observations, evidence, hypotheses, and conclusions.

rejectedCandidates.name: crafting-transformation-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: survival-crafting-domain already declares crafting-domain-service-kit plus recipe tables, inputs, outputs, stations, and failure rules.

rejectedCandidates.name: shelter-exposure-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: survival-needs and weather domain services already own temperature and exposure descriptors, while generic resource/pressure kits own the reusable scalar transitions.

rejectedCandidates.name: crowd-flow-demand-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Current NexusEngine already exports promoted OccupantFlowKit and RequestQueueKit operations semantics; a new ProtoKit would be a rename without a distinct higher-level responsibility.

rejectedCandidates.name: formation-assignment-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The existing Agent Group domain explicitly owns groups, agents, goals, basic movement, and formation state; a slot-assignment-only candidate did not clear the adjacent-domain split gate.

rejectedCandidates.name: infrastructure-restoration-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: semantic-bounded-domain-kits already installs engine.n.restoration and owns assets, diagnosis, plans, work contributions, commissioning, snapshots, and reset.

rejectedCandidates.name: stealth-suspicion-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: sensory-agent-domain-kit and perception-kit already own reusable detection observations, while rpg-social-fact-kit owns suspicion; the candidate added no coherent semantic owner above those boundaries.

rejectedCandidates.name: item-ownership-transfer-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: rpg-social-fact-kit already exposes ownership set, theft, return, crime, and suspicion facts for items; a transfer-only wrapper would duplicate that implemented surface.

rejectedCandidates.name: utility-network-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: A generic utility graph blurred electricity, water, fuel, data, repair, and service semantics. It was split down to the accepted power-distribution boundary with explicit exclusions.

rejectedCandidates.name: incident-priority-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Core RequestQueue/priority operations plus the accepted rescue-triage severity boundary already cover generic ordering and casualty priority; the candidate supplied no additional incident lifecycle meaning.

rejectedCandidates.name: signal-relay-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: acoustic-signal-domain-kit is implemented and universal-game-domain-kits already declares signal strength, sync, relay, scramble, and beacon-link surfaces; a new relay wrapper would deepen an existing overlap cluster.

rejectedCandidates.name: soil-fertility-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: agriculture-domain-kit already owns farm plot soil preparation, moisture, fertility, watering, growth, and harvest. The accepted replacement is limited to explicit nutrient-pool mass balance across farm or wild land and excludes agriculture operations.

rejectedCandidates.name: atmosphere-weather-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: atmosphere-domain-service-kit already owns renderer-agnostic fog, sky, clouds, wind, precipitation, and visibility descriptors. The accepted habitat-atmosphere boundary instead owns sealed-compartment gas inventories and breathability.

rejectedCandidates.name: flood-water-body-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: water-data-kit already declares water bodies and profiles for ponds, rivers, oceans, floods, and pools. The accepted inundation boundary owns water-volume propagation, barrier overtopping, drainage, and recession rather than another water-body record.

rejectedCandidates.name: heat-pressure-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Core Simulation and generic-pressure-loop-kit already own heat-shaped scalar resources, rates, thresholds, and transitions. The accepted thermal-energy network adds multi-zone capacity, transfer links, conservation, and equilibrium semantics.

rejectedCandidates.name: orbital-trajectory-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Core Motion owns trajectories and root-motion frames while Core Physics owns provider frames. The accepted orbital-transfer-window boundary consumes observations and owns only maneuver opportunity, reservation, and arrival-window semantics.

rejectedCandidates.name: water-utility-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: A generic water utility blurred aquifer storage, potable treatment, distribution, fluid physics, contamination, and repair. It was split into aquifer-balance and potable-water-service boundaries with explicit cross-domain facts.

rejectedCandidates.name: radiation-dose-shielding-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: generic-pressure-loop-kit explicitly owns radiation-shaped pressure channels and universal-game-domain-kits already declares radiation-hazard-kit, while the documented radiation-field candidate already names intensity, shielding, and exposure. A new dose wrapper would duplicate those surfaces.

rejectedCandidates.name: avalanche-risk-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current ProtoKit idea inventory already defines avalanche-risk-kit with snowpack stress, trigger zones, and runout. Re-accepting the same scope under a domain suffix would not be genuinely new.

rejectedCandidates.name: ecosystem-food-web-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted ecosystem-population-cycle-domain-kit already owns aggregate feeding, predation, cohort counts, carrying capacity, and trophic-flow descriptors. A food-web wrapper added no separate state owner.

rejectedCandidates.name: manufacturing-workflow-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: semantic-bounded-domain-kits already declares manufacturing and processing under its production boundary, while crafting owners already cover recipes, inputs, outputs, stations, and failure rules. The accepted material-recovery stream is limited to heterogeneous discard classification and recovery dispositions.

rejectedCandidates.name: chain-of-custody-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The existing idea inventory already declares chain-of-custody-kit and provenance-ledger-kit, and engine.n.investigation owns evidence and case meaning. The accepted specimen-integrity boundary excludes evidentiary custody and owns only physical condition, seals, aliquots, preservation exposure, and analysis readiness.

rejectedCandidates.name: ecosystem-restoration-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: A broad restoration boundary blurred interventions, work plans, habitat fit, population outcomes, succession, and content. Existing engine.n.restoration owns diagnosis and work commissioning; the accepted habitat-succession boundary owns only ecological stage transitions and alternate stable states.

rejectedCandidates.name: immune-response-lifecycle-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: Accepted contagion-transmission-domain-kit already owns subject infection stages, recovery, immunity windows, exposure lineage, and deterministic outbreak state; a second immune lifecycle would split the same subject-state owner.

rejectedCandidates.name: wildlife-migration-route-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current idea inventory already declares migration-route-kit for paths and timing and wildlife-population-kit for population groups and migration, while accepted ecosystem population and habitat corridor contracts own adjacent cohort and connectivity meaning.

rejectedCandidates.name: tidal-cycle-window-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current idea inventory already declares tidal-cycle-kit with tides and shoreline exposure. Re-accepting the same periodic access and shoreline boundary under a domain suffix would not be genuinely new.

rejectedCandidates.name: landslide-slope-stability-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current idea inventory already declares landslide-risk-kit with slope stress, debris paths, and blockage, while terrain erosion and structural support own adjacent surface and support behavior.

rejectedCandidates.name: pressure-vessel-integrity-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The current inventory already declares pressure-surge-kit, pipe-break-kit, and water-pressure-kit with pressure, valves, rupture risk, leaks, and repair needs; the proposed vessel boundary did not establish a distinct owner beyond those pressure-system semantics.

rejectedCandidates.name: weather-risk-forecast-domain-kit
rejectedCandidates.reason: The existing idea and game-domain inventories already declare weather-alert, hazard-forecast, storm-front, risk-forecast, and dependency-forecast owners. A new weather-risk wrapper would deepen the same prediction and alert overlap cluster.
